description_long: This object is a stylized metallic sculpture of a bird, constructed from thin, curved metal rods that outline the form of the body, wings, and tail. The bird stands upright on two slender legs, each ending in three forward-pointing toes and one rear toe, mimicking the stance of a large terrestrial bird. The head is elongated with a slightly pointed beak, and the neck is gracefully arched, giving the figure a dynamic, lifelike posture. The wings are partially open, with the rods fanning out to suggest feathers, while the tail is long and tapers to a rounded end. The entire structure is hollow, with significant open space between the metal elements, creating a lightweight and airy appearance. The surface of the metal has a glossy, reflective finish, with hues of silver and hints of purple or bronze depending on the lighting.
description: A glossy, metallic bird sculpture with an arched neck, open wings, and a long, rounded tail, formed from thin, curved rods.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's arched neck, pointed beak, and the full length of the tail and legs.
description_view_1: Rear three-quarter view highlighting the open, fanned wings and the hollow, airy structure of the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view focusing on the bird's profile, with the beak, neck, and tail clearly visible.
description_view_3: Back view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of the wings and the rounded tail shape.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Solid metallic rods outlining feather groups, no natural feather pattern
